title: auth — developer configuration
description: Full authentication policy — principal age gates, password policy composition, session lifecycle, lockout thresholds, JWT signing + rotation, cross-app SSO. Read alongside `config/identity.php` (owned by the identity substrate); this file OVERLAYS identity's config with auth-flow-specific keys. When a key exists in both, `auth.*` wins on the auth-flow path (which is the tenant-configurable surface); `identity.*` is the substrate default.

principal.description: Age + verification requirements on the principal being authenticated. Enforced by CompliantAgePolicy at every registration + email-change flow.

keys.description: Framework default minimum age. CompliantAgePolicy computes MAX(compliance_floor, this_value) so tenant settings + regime overrides can only TIGHTEN. Default 13 aligns with the US COPPA floor.
Minors between this age and adult transition age (18 default) can register but require guardian approval. Consumed by domain policy (sports/athletes/AthleteRegistrationPolicy in Wave 7).
Block session issuance until Identity.email_verified_at is populated. Off = accept unverified identities (rare).

password.description: Password policy for the auth-flow endpoints. Composes identity's `strong_password` rule (which owns min-length + complexity + breach check + history). Auth-specific extension keys live here.

keys.description: When identity.password.hasher upgrades (e.g. bcrypt → argon2id) OR cost parameters change, LoginAction rehashes the plaintext under the new params inside the same verify transaction. Off = keep old hashes; only new sets migrate.
When platform admin uses `identity:force-password-reset`, the target Identity is flagged so the NEXT login prompts a password change before issuing a full session.
Idle lifetime — revoke sessions that haven't touched `last_used_at` within this window. Default 8 hours.
Absolute lifetime — revoke sessions this many hours after issue regardless of activity. Default 7 days. Sanctum PATs get `expires_at` set to (now + this).
Refresh-token lifetime. Default 30 days. Rotated on every use — the family_id tracks the chain.
How far back to look for a matching device fingerprint before flagging a login as `new device`.
Master toggle for the new-device notification flow. Off = auth.detect_new_device middleware is a no-op.
Absolute lifetime when the tenant holds the `sanctum_pat_extended_ttl` entitlement (Enterprise). Overrides absolute_ttl_hours when entitled.

lockout.description: Per-endpoint rate limiting. Complements the identity substrate's per-Identity lockout (managed by identity.check_lockout middleware).

keys.description: Max login attempts per IP over the window.
Per-email rate limit on the forgot-password endpoint. Rejected requests STILL return 200 for enumeration safety.

mfa.description: MFA challenge dispatch policy. TOTP + WebAuthn verification themselves are owned by `identity/mfa`.

keys.description: How long an issued MFA challenge remains verifiable.
Terminal after this many failures. Fires MfaChallengeFailed(is_terminal=true) + hard-deletes the challenge; the caller must restart from /auth/login.
How long a successfully-verified device fingerprint is exempt from MFA re-challenge. 0 = re-challenge on every login.

jwt.description: Inter-service JWT contract. HS256 in Wave 1a; RS256/ES256 deferred to Wave 1c per design.md §12.

keys.description: Wave 1a only supports HS256. Wave 1c will add RS256, ES256.
`iss` claim on every JWT. Downstream verifiers check this.
Access-token JWT lifetime. Default 15 minutes.
Applied when the tenant holds `jwt_extended_expiry` entitlement (Enterprise). Overrides ttl_seconds.
Downstream verifiers permit +/- this many seconds of clock drift.
How long a retired signing key remains verify-capable (via JWKS publication) after rotation. Any JWT issued under the previous key must expire naturally within this window.
Whether the auth.jwt_verify middleware consults auth_jwt_deny_list. Off = trust natural expiry only (never in production).
Cache-Control header on GET /.well-known/jwks.json. Aggressive because downstream verifiers cache aggressively; rotation windows are longer than the cache TTL.

cross_app_sso.description: Grant-based cross-Application SSO. Requires cross_app_sso entitlement.

keys.description: Master toggle. Off = /auth/cross-app/* endpoints return 503. On + entitlement present = enabled per tenant.
Grant TTL. Short window — grants are one-time-use redemption tokens, not sessions.

When the target Application doesn't have a User row for this Identity, ALLOW auto-provisioning during exchange (rare — default off; when off, the exchange returns 404 and the SPA routes to the target Application's onboarding flow).

impersonation.description: Platform-staff impersonation policy. Composes with `access/delegation` (Wave 1b) for session storage.

keys.description: Hard cap on any impersonation session. Sessions extend at their own TTL until this cap.
Refuses impersonation start without a --reason flag (audit-critical).
Dispatches ImpersonationSessionActiveNotification to the target User. Default on — the impersonated user has a legal right to know.
Every mutating Artisan command in this module requires --reason=<text> (audit-logged). Off in dev; on in staging + prod.
